setting.note: A Copenhagen of black winter water and Greenlandic exile. A half-Inuit woman who reads snow the way others read print refuses to accept that a neighbour's child simply fell from a warehouse roof, and follows the cold trail from the flats of Christianshavn out through the harbour and the Freeport to a ship bound north. The map runs along the Inner Harbour waterfront and up the Østerbro shore.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Peter Høeg, first published 1992 as Frøken Smillas fornemmelse for sne. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Smilla's Sense of Snow, Peter Høeg (1992). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The glacial blue of harbour ice and a northern sky. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
